channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: An Israeli strike on a Gaza humanitarian zone tent camp kills at least 40 people, Palestinians say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on a crowded tent camp housing Palestinians displaced by the war in Gaza killed at least 40 people and wounded 60 others early Tuesday, Palestinian officials said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge asks potential jurors if they can be fair toward 3 officers charged in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Potential jurors in the federal trial of three former Memphis police officers charged with violating Tyre Nichols ’ civil rights were asked Monday whether heavy media coverage and publicly released video of him being beaten to death could influence their ability to be fair. U.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The Harris-Trump debate becomes the 2024 election's latest landmark event
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kamala Harris and Donald Trump will meet for the first time face-to-face Tuesday night for perhaps their only debate, a high-pressure opportunity to showcase their starkly different visions for the country after a tumultuous campaign summer. The event, at 9 p.m.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kentucky shooting suspect planned to 'kill a lot of people': Affidavit
item.description: As the search for a man who's considered armed and dangerous continues to unfold, court documents have revealed more about what led to Saturday's shooting on I-75.
item.dc:creator: Madylin Goins, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ charges leaders of white supremacist ‘Terrorgram’ chat group accused of encouraging hate crimes, murders
item.description: Two people are facing federal charges for their alleged involvement in a white supremacist Telegram group that was used to spread hateful content and stir up a race war, the Department of Justice said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video shows officers yank Tyreek Hill out of car as altercation quickly escalates
item.description: A police officer dragged Miami Dolphins wide receiver Tyreek Hill out of his sports car by his arm and head and then forced him face first onto the ground.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What we know about rapper Rich Homie Quan's death
item.description: An autopsy was performed Friday on Quan, but it could take months before medical examiner releases cause of death.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-officer's lies led to couple's death in Houston drug raid, prosecutor tells jurors
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A former Houston police officer is responsible for the 2019 deaths of a couple during a raid of their home because his lies on a search warrant wrongly portrayed them as dangerous drug dealers, a prosecutor told jurors on Monday.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harvey Weinstein taken to NYC hospital for emergency heart surgery: representatives
item.description: Jailed ex-movie mogul Harvey Weinstein underwent emergency heart surgery at a New York City hospital on Monday, his representatives said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Data indicates voting by noncitizens is rare. Republicans are pressing measures to make sure of it
item.description: Only U.S. citizens are eligible to vote in this fall's election for president and other top offices. While that's nothing new, the potential for noncitizens to register or vote has been receiving a lot of attention lately. Citing an influx of immigrants in recent years at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New iPhone 16 will have 2 more buttons: Here's what they do
item.description: Seven years after controversially ditching the home button, Apple is adding buttons back to its newest iPhone models.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Schools shut down amid manhunt for Kentucky highway shooting suspect
item.description: More than a dozen school districts shut down Monday across a wide swath of southeastern Kentucky as a grueling search for Joseph A. Couch, the man suspected of opening fire on an interstate highway and wounding five people over the weekend, stretched into a third day.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A 9/11 anniversary tradition is handed down to a new generation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A poignant phrase echoes when 9/11 victims' relatives gather each year to remember the loved ones they lost in the terror attacks. “I never got to meet you.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Mormon church's oldest-ever president has turned 100
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Leaders of worldwide religions commonly stay at the helm well past retirement age, but it’s not often you find centenarians overseeing major faiths. That’s what The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints has as of Monday when church president Russell M. Nelson turned 100.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia officers increase presence at Barrow County schools as students prepare to return
item.description: Georgia police will ramp up their presence at schools across Barrow County as students begin returning to classes Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge orders change of venue in trial of man charged with killing 4 University of Idaho students
item.description: A judge has agreed to move the trial of man charged in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students to a different city.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE and GENE JOHNSON Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Rescuers save man trapped in 36-inch drainpipe
item.description: A harrowing rescue unfolded amid sweltering temperatures as first responders in Ventura County, California, worked to free a man from a 36-inch drainpipe Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LL Flooring reverses course and will keep hundreds of stores under new owner
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After securing a last-minute buyer, LL Flooring is reversing course on shutting down all of its stores. The hardwood flooring retailer formerly known as Lumber Liquidators signed an agreement with private equity firm F9 Investments for a sale of its business late last week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Thousands evacuate as wildfires rage outside Los Angeles and Reno, Nevada
item.description: Thousands of people have been evacuated from the path of a scorching wildfire in the foothills of a national forest east of Los Angeles as the blaze threatens some 36,000 structures.
item.dc:creator: Eugene Garcia And Trân Nguyễn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Big Lots files for bankruptcy, plans to close hundreds of locations
item.description: Popular discount retailer Big Lots has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. As part of the bankruptcy and restructuring, 295 stores have already begun to shut down in what the company says is "optimizing our store footprint.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KTLA 5

item.title: Group launches get-out-the-vote campaign centered on people with medical debt
item.description: An estimated 100 million people in the United States have medical or dental bills they are paying off or that are overdue.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: A look at some of the oldest religious leaders in the world
item.description: The top leader of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints is celebrating a milestone birthday, his 100th. Like the president of the Utah-based faith — commonly known as the Mormon church — leaders of religions worldwide commonly stay at the helm well past normal retirement age.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kentucky manhunt enters third day as officers hack through thickets with machetes: 'Like a jungle'
item.description: Joseph Couch, a suspect in the I-75 shooting in southeastern Kentucky, remains on the loose Monday as authorities search a heavily wooded area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Five House Democrats to watch in the fight over government funding
item.description: The fight over government funding is officially underway — and all eyes will be on five Democrats in tight races who supported a controversial GOP proposal over the summer that is now attached to a stopgap meant to avert a shutdown. The gambit from Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Many student loan recipients aren’t making payments: report
item.description: Some say they can't afford the payments, but many others say they're holding out for another round of forgiveness by Uncle Sam.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, KTLA 5

item.title: Tropical system expected to strengthen near Mexico and Texas and bring heavy rains, forecasters say
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A tropical system in the southwestern Gulf of Mexico was expected to strengthen this week into a tropical storm and dump heavy rains onto Mexico and Texas before reaching the U.S. as a potential hurricane, the National Weather Service said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Shohei Ohtani hits 46th homer to tie career high on sweltering day at Dodger Stadium
item.description: Shohei Ohtani hit his 46th home run of the season to tie his career high, a 450-foot solo shot down the right-field line in the fifth inning of the Los Angeles Dodgers' game against the Cleveland Guardians on a sweltering Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rare dime hidden for decades could bring huge payout for Ohio farm family
item.description: Three sisters from Ohio who inherited a dime kept in a bank vault for more than 40 years knew it had some value. But they had no idea just how much until just a few years ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tennessee man cited for having too many flags in yard
item.description: A Franklin resident is asking city leaders to reconsider an ordinance after he received a citation for having too many flags in his front yard.
item.dc:creator: Nikki McGee, KRON4

item.title: September tax filing deadline looms – except in these disaster areas
item.description: The September deadline to make an estimated tax payment is fast approaching, but taxpayers living in designated disaster zones across the U.S. will be granted an automatic delay, according to the IRS.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Rapper Kendrick Lamar to headline Super Bowl LIX halftime show
item.description: Kendrick Lamar is returning to the Super Bowl halftime show - he was part of a legendary group of hip hop artists we saw perform in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Harris falling behind among male voters in key states
item.description: New polls show Vice President Harris faces a major challenge in winning over male voters and is losing men by a bigger margin than she’s winning women in key states such as Pennsylvania, Nevada and North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: When can customers expect the next Costco price hike?
item.description: When can customers expect the next price hike? The former CFO had previously laid out a possible timeline.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Kentucky interstate shooting: Suspect at large as investigators find gun, SUV
item.description: While a car and firearm have been located, investigators are continuing to search for the suspect in a Saturday night shooting that injured five people in rural Kentucky.
item.dc:creator: Corey Elam, Fox 5

item.title: Mother of Georgia shooting suspect called school to warn of emergency, family says
item.description: The mother of the 14-year-old who has been charged with murder over the fatal shooting of four people at his Georgia high school called the school before the killings, warning staff of an “extreme emergency” involving her son, a relative said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Evacuations ordered as wildfire burns in foothills of national forest east of LA
item.description: SAN BERNARDINO, Calif. (AP) — Evacuations were ordered Saturday as a wildfire scorched the foothills of a national forest east of Los Angeles, amid a days-long heat wave that pushed temperatures into the triple digits across the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina judge halts mailing of ballots with RFK Jr's name on them
item.description: A North Carolina judge on Friday halted the state from sending out mail-in ballots that still have Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s name on them, delaying the start of voting.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, Fox 5
